## Highlights

- **Nomad Back button moved to the leading edge** — the iOS-supplied left-side `<` used to pop the whole page view straight back to the Nomad directory, while the in-page Back (history-aware) sat alone on the right. Tester report: "click a result → it correctly takes me to the linked page, but Back takes me to the list of nomad pages instead of back to the search results." The leading slot is now ours: tap goes to the prior page first, falls through to the directory only when the in-page history is empty.
- **Back replays form submits** (parity with `android-v1.2.15`) — search-results pages are POST-driven, and the previous Back pop was re-fetching their path as a plain GET, so the user landed on an empty form instead of the actual results. Each history entry now carries the POST data that produced the page; Back replays the submit so a cross-node round-trip (search site → results → result page on site A → Back) returns to the populated results rather than the empty search engine.
